extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), whose condition is the conjunction of 12 inputs including pregnancy. rec_drug_b is set true only by r2 (priority 50), whose condition is pregnancy alone. The r3 condition is a strict superset of the r2 condition, so whenever r3 fires (making rec_drug_a true), r2 necessarily also fires (making rec_drug_b true) because pregnancy is required by both. No priority or tie-breaking issue blocks this: r3 is the sole rule touching rec_drug_a and r2 the sole rule touching rec_drug_b, so each wins its own output, and defaults do not apply since both outputs are explicitly set. r1 and r4 only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. Setting all 12 conjuncts of r3 to true (and the two unrelated inputs anticoagulated/g6pd_deficiency false) makes both r3 and r2 fire: rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true, violating mutual exclusion.
